titleStmt.title: Saint Petersburg, Institut Vostočnyh Rukopisej Rossijskoj Akademii Nauk, IV Suhtelen 255
Miracles of Mary

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msIdentifier.repository: Institut Vostočnyh Rukopisej Rossijskoj Akademii Nauk

msItem.title: Taʾammǝra Māryām
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of sick people
msItem.incipit: ኵሎሙ፡ ድዉያን፡ እለ፡ መጽኡ፡ ኀበ፡ ውእቱ፡ ቤት፡ እምነ፡ ቤተ፡ ልሔም፡ ወአድያሚሃ፡ እምድረ፡ ይሁዳ።
msItem.note: The miracle is not accounted for by Boris Turaev.

This miracle is identified by Miracles of MaryWilliamMacombern.d.117 as Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, the apostles convene around the Virgin, however the description and incipit provided do not a allow a certain identification before consultation of the manuscript.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Tomās for the 21st Ṭǝrr
msItem.incipit: ወካዕበ፡ ንነግረክሙ፡ መንክረ፡ ዘጸሐፋ፡ አባ፡ ቶማስ፡ ቀሲስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 116. An account written by priest Tomas as to how an infidel wanted to pierce the image of St. Mary on 21 Ṭǝrr and about the conversion of the latter's servant

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the transmigration of the Virgin, Tāfonyā is sent to break the Virgin's bed and is saved by her
msItem.note: Numbered 117. A story about the Jew Tāfoniyā.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: History of the Church of the Virgin in Zǝwilā
msItem.note: Numbered 118. An account of the miracles at the Egyptian church of Ḥārat Zuwāylah.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary when at Bethlehem healed king Safron and drove out devils
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ንጉሠ፡ ግብጽ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ሰፍሮን፡
msItem.note: Numbered 119. Healing of the Egyptian king Safron from a lion's bite, healing of two mad women, etc.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the scribe Damianus from Philippi
msItem.explicit: ወይቤሎ፡ ለጳጳስ፡ ብከኑ፡ እብነ፡ ጸዓዳ፡ ወጽዱለ፡ ወአምሮ፡ ትእም
msItem.note: Numbered 120. Miracle of the scribe Damianus from Philippi. According to Enrico Cerulli, the miracle is mutilated.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: The beggar at the church of Qirqos in ʾAmhārā from whose head a horn grew
msItem.incipit: ምንዳቤሆሙ፡ ለክርስቲያን፡ ወይእቲ፡ ረዳኢቶሙ፡ ለሰማዕት፡ ወለመላእክት፡
msItem.note: According to E. Cerulli, the account is acephalous and mutilated at the end; it is a story of a healing of a poor Ethiopian on whose elbow a "horn" grew up. St. Mary appeared to him in a vision (missing in the manuscript), he prayed to her and the "horn" fell off on the eve of the feast of 21 Gǝnbot

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Phoenician evil man called Bārok
msItem.incipit: እግዚእትነ፡ ቅድስት፡ ድንግል፡ ማርያም፡ ወላዲተ፡ አምላክ፡ ጸሎታ፡ ወበረከታ፡ የሀሉ፡ ምስለ፡ ኩልነ፡ ውሉደ፡ ጥምቀት፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን።
msItem.note: Indicated by Boris Turaev and supplied with number 139, but this is uncertain. According to E. Cerulli, a part in the beginning is missing.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Bishop Philoteus
msItem.note: Numbered 140. The miracle of Bishop Philoteus.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Anastasius, a deacon of Rome
msItem.note: Numbered 141.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jew from Jerusalem swallowed by a snake
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ብእሲ፡ ዕብራዊ፡ እምዘመደ፡ ክቡራን፡
msItem.note: Numbered 142. While angling, a Jew from Jerusalem was swallowed by a "snake". He was saved three days later, after a prayer to St. Mary. He was baptized in the river of Sāliḥom, was cleansed from leprosy and remained a servant of the church of St. Mary till the end of his life.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary obtained forgiveness for a monk who mutilated himself after he had defiled himself
msItem.note: Numbered 143. Resurrection of the monk Bārdǝyos from the country of Bānyos, who made a sin while he was on his way to ābbā Yāʿqob and, instigated by Devil, committed suicide.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How a bishop removed a priest from his office and how the Virgin Mary rebuked the bishop
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ብእሲ፡ ካህን፡ ወቀሲስ፡ በሀገረ፡ ቂሳርያ፡ ዘስሙ፡ እንድርያስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 144. A Caesarean presbyter Andrew celebrated the liturgy of St. Mary; the community complained about this to Bishop Dorotheus who first prohibited Andrew to do so, but repented after the apparition of St. Mary.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How a shoemaker's daughter called Mary was taken possession of a horse-solder, who spared her virginity
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ብእሲ፡ በሀገረ፡ ደማስቆስ፡ ዘስሙ፡ አቢፋንዮስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 145. The cobbler Epifanius of Damascus had a daughter Mary who dedicated herself to the Mother of God. Also she made to Her servant the patrician Nifon (Niphontus), or ʾAgābos. He brought Mary to the monastery of Kalamon, made big donations and bequeathed himself to be buried there. When he was killed in the oasis ʾElwāḥ, St. Mary appeared to the abbess and ordered her to go to the place of Nifon's death and transport his body to the monastery.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jewish boy from Tyrus, saved by Mary from the furnace
msItem.explicit: ወእሉ፡ ተጠምቁ፡ በይእቲ፡ ዕለት፡ ምስሌ፡ ዮሴፍ፡ አርባዕቱ፡ ምእት፡ ነፍስ፡ እምሰብአ፡ ቤቱ፡ ዘእንበለ፡ እ
msItem.note: Numbered 146. The end is missing.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: The Story of the Cannibal of Qǝmr
msItem.incipit: ወይሰቲ፡ ማየ፡ ወቀስተሂ፡ ዘስርግው፡ በወርቅ፡ ወእንዘ፡ የሐውር፡ ረከበ፡ አሐዱ፡ ሐረሳዊ፡
msItem.note: The beginning is missing.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary, when twelve years old, healed an epileptic
msItem.incipit: ወነበረት፡ ዐሠርተ፡ ወከልኤተ፡ ዓመተ፡ እንዘ፡ ትትናዘዝ፡ እምኀበ፡ መላእክት፡
msItem.note: Numbered 123. Healing of an epileptic man to appealed to St. Mary when she was yet at the temple. The identification is uncertain.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a thief merchant
msItem.incipit: ወካዕበ፡ ኮነ፡ አሐዱ፡ ነጋዲ፡ በሀገረ፡ ቂሳርያ፡ ወዝንቱ፡ አይሁዳዊ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 124. An account of Basil (the Great?) of Caesarea about conversion of a Jewish merchant who stole a golden chalice from a church, and the apparition of St. Mary to him. After the conversion, his name was Sutāy or Šutāy.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a fountain of water caused by Jesus in Māḫḍāb while returning to Palestine
msItem.note: Numbered 125. The miracle of a fountain of water caused by Jesus.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of St Minas
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩መኰንን፡ በሀገረ፡ ነቅስዮስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 126. An account about the birth of St. Minas to the city governor Eudoxius, after his wife prayed in front of the image of St. Mary.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of ʾabbā Matewos who wounded his tongue to escape from being nominated patriarch
msItem.note: Numbered 127.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Mark, son of the king of Rome, Gabra ʾIyasus
msItem.note: Numbered 128. Identification according to Il codice di Leningrado del libro etiopico dei Miracoli di MariaAtti della Accademia nazionale dei Lincei, Rendiconti, Classe di scienze morale, storiche e filologicheEnricoCerulli1965Serie ottava, 201-19no. 22

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a woman who despite her mother-in-law adored a torn image of Mary
msItem.note: Numbered 129.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a monk of the Monastery of St Samuel of Kalamon
msItem.note: Numbered 130.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the flight of Egypt; Miracle of the sycomore of Dabra Mǝṭmāq
msItem.incipit: ዘገብረቶ፡ እግዝእትነ፡ ማርያም፡ ምስለ፡ ፍቁር፡ ወልዳ፡
msItem.explicit: አመረ፡ ኀበ፡ ዮሴፍ፡ ወወሀቦ፡ ኪያሃ፡ በትረ፡ ወእምድኅረ፡ ነሥአ፡ ወይቤላ
msItem.note: Numbered 131.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a pregnant woman
msItem.note: Numbered 147.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a shipwrecked pilgrim
msItem.incipit: ወሀለወት፡ አሐቲ፡ ሐመር፡ ዓቢይ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 148. Pilgrims and Bishop Gregory sailed on the Black Sea in a ship. They were shipwrecked, but a pilgrim who had always addressed St. Mary in his prayers was miraculously saved.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Jonah, his wife and another woman
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ብእሲ፡ በሀገረ፡ ፍልስጥኤም፡ ዘስሙ፡ ዮናስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 149. In Palestine, a man called Jonah forsook his wife and went to another woman. The wife prayed in front of an image of St. Mary asking that her rival be afflicted with all kinds of disasters. She saw a dream in which St. Mary told her that her request is not fitting, and that the rival venerates St. Mary as well and attends the church daily. Finally, the rival woman repented and left Jonah.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Dina resurrected and released from the Hades by St. Mary
msItem.incipit: ወኮነ፡ ልማዶሙ፡ ለሰብአ፡ ሮሜ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 150. In Rome, on the day of the Assumption of St. Mary, during a procession, a woman suddenly met her female friend Dinā, a daughter of ʾAwsānyā. She had died, had been resurrected from the dead by St. Mary and released from the Hades where she was placed as punishment for her dissolute behaviour.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Virgin Mary and the Lady Euphemia
msItem.note: Numbered 151.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the youth Zechariah, from Rome
msItem.note: Numbered 152.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Juliana and Barbara from Bethlehem
msItem.note: Numbered 153. According to Il codice di Leningrado del libro etiopico dei Miracoli di MariaAtti della Accademia nazionale dei Lincei, Rendiconti, Classe di scienze morale, storiche e filologicheEnricoCerulli1965Serie ottava, 201-19no. 33, this is the "long version" that includes the name Bethlehem.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Theodosia, sister of Constantine, who found the cross in Jerusalem
msItem.note: Numbered 154.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary sent a vision to a young man and caused him to embrace the ascetic life
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ብእሲ፡ በሀገረ፡ ባርቶስ፡ ወልድ፡ ወሬዛ፡ ወስሙ፡ ቆዝሞስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 155. Cosmas of Bārtos (Beirut?) venerated the Mother of God. He went to the monastery on the Mount of Olives and withdrew into a cave. The abbot told him to reside in the community. Cosmas prayed in the night in front of the image of St. Mary, received a call to serve her in a dream, and took the monastic vow.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of an officer of Ephesus who was innocently crucified and saved by Mary
msItem.note: Numbered 156.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel
msItem.note: Numbered 157.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How a widow forsook her duties in the Church and lived in sin, how her place had been filled by the Virgin Mary
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ደሴት፡ መንገለ፡ ባሕረ፡ ኢያሪኮ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 158. There was a monastery on an island in the Sea of ʾIyāriko; one of the nuns venerating the Mother of God fell in love and run away with a priest. After four years she repented, and St. Mary brought her back into her cell so that the others did not notice, protecting her from the evil tongues.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary and her Son appeared in a beehive
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ብእሲ፡ ሐያቤ፡ መዓር፡
msItem.note: Numbered 159. A beekeeper consulted a sorcerer (how to get more honey from the bees); following her advice, he put into a beehive the holy communion that he brought in his mouth.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jew of ʾAḫmīm
msItem.note: Numbered 160.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the scribe Damianus from Philippi
msItem.note: Numbered 161. Miracle of the scribe Damianus from Philippi (repeated?).

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Bishop of Rome, ʾAbbās (Leo)
msItem.note: Numbered 162.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of ʾArsimā, whom Mary announced that two girls would be exempted from wordly desires
msItem.note: Numbered 163.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a sinful woman who swallowed a scorpion
msItem.incipit: ወሀለወት፡ አሐቲ፡ ብእሲት፡ ኃጥእት፡
msItem.note: Numbered 164. A sinful woman wanted to commit suicide and swallowed a scorpion. Suffering from great pain, she put on her breast an image of St. Mary. After that she gave birth to three scorpions, recovered and confessed her sins to archpriest John "according to the law of the Synodos".

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary administered to certain monks a celestial compound to digest their food
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ብእሲ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ሲላን፡ ዘሀገረ፡ ኢቆንዮስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 165. A vision of the monk Silon in the country of Iqonyos: the Mother of God, appearing in the meal time, prevents a harm from poor food of the monk who feed on wild plants.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary rebuked a woman who gave up fasting at her husband's command
msItem.incipit: ወሀለወት፡ አሐቲ፡ ብእሲት፡ በሀገረ፡ ሮሜ፡ በቤተ፡ ሶፍያ፡ ዘስማ፡ እንባብሬና፡
msItem.explicit: In Rome, ʾƎnbābrenā lived in the house of Sophia. She had an eye disease, and fastened according to the vow she gave to the Mother of God. The faith of her husband was not big; he forced her to break the fasting. The Mother of God awarded the woman on each and filled her chest with precious jewelry. The woman understood and managed to convince her husband. The chest became enmpty again.
msItem.note: Numbered 166.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Sophia who distributed all her jewelry to the poor, and her husband who did not let her go to the church
msItem.incipit: ወሀለወት፡ አሐቲ፡ ብእሲት፡ ዘስማ፡ ሶፍያ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 167. Sophia was from a noble Israelite lineage and her husband was from the "lineage of Aaron". They lived on an island in the Northern Sea. The confessor Mark advised them to deny the luxury for the sake of the poor. The wife distributed all her jewelries. The husband was angry and did not permit the wife to go to the church on the feast of Presentation of Mary in the Temple, because the clothes of her wife were poor. Then she started to pray at home and saw the heavenly liturgy in a dream.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary and our Lord became sureties to a Jew for the debt owed to him by a certain merchant of Constantinople
msItem.incipit: ወነበረ፡ ውስተ፡ ቍስጥንጥንያ፡ ዘስሙ፡ አቦሊ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 168. A story about the tradesman ʾAboli from Constantinople. He borrowed money from the Jew Gardān on the day of St. Mary's nativity, having left his son as gage, and went for trade to the shores of the "northern Ṭegros". On the way back, having reached ʾAkko, he realised that he will not be in time for repaying the debt. A delay would cause the enslavement of his son. Having prayed to the Mother of God, ʾAboli threw a box with money and a brief to the Jew into the sea. The Jew received the money but nevertheless he sued ʾAboli. The voice of the Mother of God, who was appealed to be a witness, compelled him to get baptized.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the drunk monk Timotewos (?), saved by the Virgin
msItem.note: Numbered 169.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How a woman consigned to the Devil the son she conceived on Easter eve
msItem.incipit: ወነበረ፡ አሐዱ፡ ብእሲ፡ ክቡር፡ ወቡሩክ፡ ዘይፈርሆ፡ ለእግዚአብሔር፡ ዘስሙ፡ ሰርጊስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 170. The wife and the husband broke the vow of virginity and gave birth to a son. Because of the mother's unwary words, the boy was entrusted to the Satan. He went to Rome where Pope Epiphanius gave him a letter to Bishop Niqonyos of Jerusalem. The latter sent the boy to the monastery of Dabra Qʷānṣǝl, to the ascetic Gabra Krǝstos. He entrusted the boy to the Mother of God, who saved him from Hell when Gabra Krǝstos he was celebrating the Mass for him.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary protected a philantropic merchant who had beggared himself
msItem.incipit: ወነበረ፡ ብእሲ፡ ባዕል፡ ወነጋዲ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ፊልጶስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 171. A rich trader Philipp lived in the city of Niqonyā; he distributed all his possession among the poor. Another trader, Silān, lent him 1,000 golden coins, but Philipp used them for charitable purposes. Silān got angry about this but repented when he saw the Last Judgement at which the Mother of God is trying to save him, referring to the money given for good things.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary and St Peter saved from hell the souls of Peter the deacon and Stephen, two brothers from Constantinople
msItem.incipit: ወሀለዉ፡ ክልኤቱ፡ አኃው፡ በሀገረ፡ ሮሜ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 173. Two brothers lived in Rome, archdeacon Peter, and Stephan, the governor of the city of ʾƎnṣǝnā. Both were greedy; Stephan robbed the church of St. Sirloryos but celebrated the commemoration of St. Peter the Apostle, and fed the poor on that day. After he died, St. Peter reconciled him with Sirloryos and presented him to the Mother of God, who managed to get for him a three day resurrection for recompensing the living people whom he offended. Thanks to the prayers of Stephan and Pope Fināḥas, Peter obtain the same chance.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary delivered from hell the soul of an evil man who had intended to build a church in her name
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ፈረሳዊ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ማልኮስ፡ በሀገረ፡ ንሒስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 170. The knight Mālkos of the city of Nǝḥis, being a sinner, monthly celebrated the feasts of St. Mary and Archangel Michael. Later he repented, intended to found a monastery, but died. Archangel Michael and St. Mary saved his soul from demons and returned it into his body. He lived 88 years after that.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How a bishop from St Peter's church in Rome stole holy oil and bought two pearls for Mary after having been rebuked
msItem.incipit: ወነበረ፡ በሀገረ፡ ሮሜ፡ ሊቀ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ ዲበ፡ መንበረ፡ ጴጥሮስ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ጰኵሚስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 171. Pope Pachomius stole myrrh from his own church and sold it. Apostle Peter threatened him with Gehenna. Pachomius gathered the priests, and one old man said to him that the only way of salvation is to pray to the Mother of God. First the prayers of Peter were rejected, his sin was pardoned only after the third prayer thanks to the mediation of St. Mary. With the money received for myrrh, Pachomius bought a precious stone and decorated an image of the Mother of Got with it.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How an avaricious priest refused to leave the bed of a rich man to go to a dying woman
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ቀሲስ፡ ውስተ፡ አሐቲ፡ ሀገር፡ እምብሔረ፡ ግብጽ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 172. An account about a greedy Egyptian priest. He refused to visit a dying old woman who venerated the Mother of God, and went to a dying money lender instead.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary saved a young widow from a knight, who became a monk
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ደብር፡ ውስተ፡ አሐቲ፡ ሀገር፡ ዘስማ፡ ደብረ፡ ምጥማቅ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 173. An account about a nun from the Egyptian monastery of Dabra Mǝṭmāq. She was saved by the Mother of God from a sin with a rich young man, who himself repented, dispersed his possessions and went into a monastery. The miracle seems to very close or nearly identical to the one edited E.W. Budge but, in contrast to it, names the country (Egypt).

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary converted a demented herdsman and delivered him from Satan
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ውስተ፡ ሀገረ፡ ሮሜ፡ አመ፡ መዋዕለ፡ ንጉሠ፡ ሳባ፡ ፈተውኩ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 174. An account about the shepherd Paul of Rome who gave his soul to the Devil and was saved in the cathedral of St. Paul, outside of the city.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a monk in the monastery of St. Ephrem, on an island in the river of Tigris
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ በማእከለ፡ ባሕረ፡ ጤግሮስ፡ ደሴት፡ ዘይሰመይ፡ ደብረ፡ ኤፍሬም፡
msItem.note: Numbered 175. An account the blessed death of a monk who venerated the Mother of God, in a monastery of an island in the river of Tigris.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How a knight loved a lady and prayed to Mary who appeared to him and how he became a monk
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ፈረሳዊ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ኒፎን፡ ዘሃገረ፡ ገላአድ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 176. The knight Nifon of Galāʾad, suffering from an unshared love, recited each day 150 times the Praise of Mary following an advice of the monk ʾAlāniqos. After a year, while hunting, he found an old church prayed there in front of the image of the Mother of God. He appeared to him; he took on monastic habit and died one year later.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Another version of the story of Nathan the monk
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ብእሲ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ናታን፡ በደብረ፡ ሰራብዮን፡
msItem.note: Numbered 177. The knight Natan from the monastery of Seraphion venerated the Mother of God and was asking her to reveal her glory to him. She appeared to him in the midnight, with the Book of Prophet Isaiah in her hand, and let Natan touch one of her cheeks. The next day he felt bewitched; he could not sing in the church and was beaten up for this by the abbot. According to Il codice di Leningrado del libro etiopico dei Miracoli di MariaAtti della Accademia nazionale dei Lincei, Rendiconti, Classe di scienze morale, storiche e filologicheEnricoCerulli1965Serie ottava, 201-19no. 58 the text in the manuscript represents the "long version" of the miracle

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the merchant who was offered riches for denying the faith
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ አሐዱ፡ ነጋዲ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ጥብርያኖስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 178. An account about the trader Ṭǝbrǝyānos who got impoverished. Devil promised him riches if he would deny his faith. Ṭǝbrǝyānos denied Christ, angels and Baptism, but did not deny the Mother of God, "the queen of the entire world". Devil disappeared and Ṭǝbrǝyānos, after a long prayer on the remains of an old church, saw a dream in which St. Mary was supplicating for him. Ṭǝbrǝyānos woke up and went to take on the monastic habit.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary caused a storm to cease and saved sheep and took the shepherd to Paradise
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ብእሲ፡ ኖላዊ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 179. An account about a monastery shepherd who prayed to the Mother of God for saving the cattle from the cold. She appeared to him and announced that his death is coming.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary with shrouded face was seen by a collector of firewood who acquired wealth through her protection
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ብእሲ፡ ወሬዛ፡ ዘይነብር፡ ውስተ፡ አሐቲ፡ ሀገር፡
msItem.note: Numbered 180. An account about a poor young man who earned his life selling fire wood. He was awarded with richness for his reverence to the image of the Mother of God that was on the city gates.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the image of the Virgin Mary in a Church bowed to the monks
msItem.note: Numbered 181.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a brigand who loved Mary and whose funeral she commanded to the bishop
msItem.note: Numbered 182.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a picture of Mary speaking to a worker
msItem.note: Numbered 183.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Bishop of Rome, ʾAbbās (Leo)
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ወሬዛ፡ እመዋዕለ፡ ተንበላት፡
msItem.note: Numbered 184. An account about a Muslim who honoured the Mother of God. She had him converted into Christianity by Pope Leon. After the death of the latter he was elected to become pope. During his first liturgy his former girlfriend bit his hand which he cut off. Thanks to the prayer of St. Mary, he got his hand back in "shiny appearance". The miracle seems to be the same as the one above, numbered 42.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a baptized Jewish boy called Gǝrānin saved by Mary from fire
msItem.note: Numbered 185.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary raised a dead knight to fight against Golyād
msItem.note: Numbered 186.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How an Ishmaelite was converted when he saw oil and milk flowing from the breasts of a picture of the Virgin Mary
msItem.note: Numbered 187.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary appeared to a young man and made him swear he would not marry and how he ran away after his father chose a bride and broke his neck
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ንጉሥ፡ ዘይነብር፡ ውስተ፡ ባሕር፡
msItem.note: Numbered 188. An account about a prince of an island. He revered an image of St. Mary that was in the church of his house and it conversed with the prince about the necessity to remain virgin. Being compelled to marry the prince run away from home, accidentally fell and died.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary obtained forgiveness for a spendthrift because though he denied the Father, Christ, the Holy Ghost and the angels he would not deny her
msItem.note: Numbered 189. A version of (the miracle numbered 178) . A young nobleman who lived "in the middle of a sea" and got impoverished, became rich thanks to St. Mary, when a rich man who married his daughter.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary saved a lady from adultery by causing her to read the Prayers for the Dead and appeared to her
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ብእሲ፡ ባዕል፡ ጥቀ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 190. An account about a woman who was waiting for her lover and reading the book of "the Horologium of St. Mary", but she was prevented from the sin by an apparition of St. Mary.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a nobleman who established a hospital and took Satan as assistant
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ብእሲ፡ ሊቅ፡ ወባዕል፡ ጥቀ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 191. An account about a pious nobleman who established a hospital and a homeless shelter. The envious Satan became his assistant and was about to destroy him. The nobleman was saved thanks to the power of a prayer to St. Mary that he was reading daily. The prayer was recorded by a bishop who was passing by; he managed to Satan.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary delivered from the devils the soul of an avaricious farmer who had given alms in her name
msItem.note: Numbered 192.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a sinful abbot who appears a year after his death to announce his salvation by Mary
msItem.note: Numbered 193.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jew of Constantinople punished for throwing out an image of Mary
msItem.note: Numbered 194.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary forgave a lady who commited incest with her son and murdered their child
msItem.incipit: ይቤ፡ ወሀለወ፡ ውስተ፡ ሀገረ፡ ሮሜ፡ ብእሲ፡ ክቡር፡
msItem.note: Numbered 195. An account about a woman who had an incestuous relation with her son. She was pardoned thanks to the prayer of the Mother of God.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a prince saved by Mary after being punished for having wrongly assigned an abbot
msItem.note: Numbered 196.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How a young man placed a ring on the finger of a figure of the Virgin Mary, who regarded it as a ring of betrothal
msItem.note: Numbered 197.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of sunken ship saved by Mary
msItem.note: Numbered 198.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the painted figures of Christ and the Virgin Mary bled when a Jew cast the board into a pit, how the magistrate burned the Jew alive
msItem.note: Numbered 199.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a knight who denied Jesus but not Mary and was saved by her intercession
msItem.note: Numbered 200.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: How the Virgin Mary saved the soul of a monk who was crossing a stream to visit a lover
msItem.note: Numbered 201.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a sinful widow who went to hell and later appeared at her convent and announced her salvation by Mary
msItem.note: Numbered 202.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a widow whom Mary told never to recite the greeting of the angel in hurry
msItem.note: Numbered 203.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the resuscitation of the son of Tiberius, emperor of Rome
msItem.note: Numbered 204.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the resuscitation of the son of Tiberius, emperor of Rome
msItem.incipit: በእንተ፡ ዘከመ፡ አስተርአይዎ፡ ለአባ፡ ማርዳሪ፡ ፲፪መላእክት፡
msItem.note: Numbered 205. ʾAbbā Mārdāri is visited and blessed by 12 angels who were on their way to Constantinople, to listen to John Chrysostom.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a man without virtue who loved Mary
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ብእሲ፡ በአሐቲ፡ ሀገር፡ ወውእቱሰ፡ ብእሲ፡ ባዕል፡ ውእቱ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 206. Account about the salvation of a rich man’s estate from pest. The Mother of God appeared as a dove and suppressed the illness with her wings.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Thecla is taken to Alexandria to see her brother, ʾabbā ʾEsi, in a boat guided by the Archangels Gabriel and Sureyal
msItem.note: Numbered 207.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: St Mary incenses Thecla
msItem.note: Numbered 208.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the martyr ʾAbbā ʾEsi and his sister Tekla of Busir in Egypt
msItem.note: Numbered 209.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of St Paul who sees the glory of the Virgin Mary
msItem.incipit: ይቤ፡ ጳውሎስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 210. Enraptured to the Third Heaven, Paul the Apostle sees the celestial glory of St. Mary.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Bǝḥor, a disciple of ʾAmmoni, who sees the glory of the Virgin Mary
msItem.incipit: ወኮነ፡ ሎቱ፡ ለአባ፡ አሞኒ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ረድእ፡ ዘስሙ፡ አባ፡ ብሖር፡
msItem.note: Numbered 211. The Mother of God appeared to ʾabbā Bǝḥor, a disciple of ʾabbā ʾAmmoni, in a desert, and showed him Christ in the Orient, in a cart with four horses.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of St Gregory the Illuminator who sees the glory of the Virgin Mary
msItem.incipit: ይቤ፡ ጎርጎርዮስ፡
msItem.note: Numbered 212. St. Gregory of Armenia sees the celestial glory of St. Mary.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Sāulā, wife of St George the Younger
msItem.note: Numbered 213.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Convent of Māḫǝw
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ በሀገረ፡ ኢየሩሳሌም፡
msItem.note: Numbered 214. In the city of Jerusalem, at a site close to Salem and Jordan where Christ dwelt while he was on flight from Herodes, John brother of Christ appears annually on the 12th of Ḫǝdar, and the Mother of God appears on the 21st of Ṭǝr. The place is hidden from the infidels.

msItem.title: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of two sons of the magician ʾAbrāsi pardoned thanks to the prayer to St. Mary
msItem.incipit: ወሀሎ፡ ፩ንጉሥ፡ በሀገር፡ ዘይብልዎ፡ ሙፃአ፡ ፀሐይ፡
msItem.explicit: ዘከመ፡ አድኅነቶሙ፡ ወባልሐቶሙ፡ ለመስርያን፡ ለአጋንንት፡ ቅኑያን፡ እለ፡ ኢተጠምቁ፡ በስመ፡ ወልዳ፡ ብርሃን። ጸሎታ
msItem.note: Numbered 215. A daughter of the king of "the Land of the Rising Sun" gave birth to the son ʾAwǝr, from the magician ʾAbrāsi. When this was uncovered eight years later, the magician escaped to Faiyum with his two sons, but died there. His children erected a column and lived on it, doing magic. The Mother of God appeared to them and asked what the want. They asked for "the immortality milk". St. Mary ordered Archangel Gabriel to kill them, but thanks to the prayer of ʾAwǝr she had mercy upon them.

handNote.desc: Turaev does not specify further qualities of the hand

item.q: ወለዛቲ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ ዘአጽሐፍክዋ፡ አነ፡ ነዳይ፡ ወመስኪን፡ መልከ፡ ጼዴቅ፡ በእንተ፡ ፍቅራ፡ ለማርያም፡ ከመ፡ ትኩነኒ፡ ለተዝካርየ፡ ወለዝክረ፡ ስምየ፤ ወለዝክረ፡ ስሞሙ፡ ለነጋድያን፡ አባውየ፡ ወአኃውየ፡ ከመ፡ ትኩን፡ ምክሐ፡ ለኢትዮጵያ፨ ኢትርስዑኒኬ፡ ኦአኃው፡ እለ፡ አንበብክሙዋ፡ ለዛቲ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ በእንተ፡ ፍቅራ፡ ለማርያም ፨ ወአነ፡ አጽሐፍክዋ፡ በረኃብ፡ ወጽምእ፡ በሃፍ፡ ወድካም፡ ወበዕርቃን፡ እስከ፡ ተመስወ፡ አባልየ፤ ወዝኵሉ፡ በጽሐ፡ ላዕሌየ፡ በእንተ፡ ፍቅራ፡ ለእግዚእትየ፡ ቅድስት፡ ድንግል፡ ማርያም ፡ ወላዲተ፡ አምላክ፡ እመ፡ ምሕረት፨ ወአነ፡ ነዳይ፡ ወመስኪን፡ ወሀብክዋ፡ ለጌተ፡ ሴማኔ፡ መቃብሪሃ፡ ለእግዚእትየ፡ ቅድስት፡ ድንግል፡ ማርያም፡ ኦእግዚእትነ፡ ጸሎትኪ፡ ወስእለትኪ፡ የሀሉ፡ ምስለ፡ ኵሎሙ፡ ሰማዕያን፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን፨ አሜን፨ ወአሜን፨ ለይኩን፨ ለይኩን፨
እሰግድ፡ ለኪ፡ ኦእግዚእትየ፡ ማርያም፡ ለፀሐየ፡ ጽድቅ፡ ሰረገላሁ። እሰግድ፡ ለኪ፡ ኦእግዚእትየ፡ ማርያም፡ ለመርዓዊ፡ ሰማያዊ፡ ጽርሑ፡

binding.decoNote: Turaev only specifies that the binding is not Ethiopian, made of parchment.

history.provenance: The name Malka Ṣedeq is indicated on f. 49r. The manuscript was acquired by Count Pjotr Suhtelen, and after the latter's death donated to the Institute by Tsar Nikolaj IIin 1837

bibl.title: Il codice di Leningrado del libro etiopico dei Miracoli di Maria
Atti della Accademia nazionale dei Lincei, Rendiconti, Classe di scienze morale, storiche e filologiche
Rukopisnaja kniga v traditsionnoj kulture Efiopii

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)
